user_direction.decision: Proceed with the safe English Core 3000 path: Oxford remains benchmark only; production source list must come from usable/open/internal sources. Include EN-GB as a first-release text/example column, but keep transcription columns EN-only.

course.transcription_scope: US English source word and US English source example only

english_variant_decision.reason: EN-GB is included for British English word/display and example text, while transcription remains EN-only to avoid a separate British pronunciation QA branch in the first release.

first_release.reason: Small first release to prove source snapshot, 54-language QA, workbook shape, and benchmark audit before scaling.

default_release_order.role: middle core: social, work, study, travel, media, abstract vocabulary
upper core: argumentation, news, education, professional and abstract meanings
later Core 5000-style extension after Core 3000 is stable

source_stack.terms_snapshot: Official NGSL page lists NGSL 1.2 as a 2809-word downloadable list and states CC BY-SA 4.0 licensing.
source_stack.attribution_text: New General Service List by Browne, C., Culligan, B., and Phillips, J. is licensed under CC BY-SA 4.0. Retrieved from https://www.newgeneralservicelist.com/new-general-service-list.
blocked_until_review: final workbook release metadata that omits attribution

source_stack.terms_snapshot: Official CEFR-J site publishes Wordlist Version 1.6. Open Language Profiles records CEFR-J vocabulary/grammar datasets as usable for research and commercial purposes with proper citation.
source_stack.attribution_text: The CEFR-J Wordlist Version 1.6. Compiled by Yukio Tono, Tokyo University of Foreign Studies. Retrieved from https://www.cefr-j.org/download.html.
blocked_until_review: production source-list use without stored snapshot and citation text

blocked: copy exact Oxford word list as source data

blocked_content: Oxford exact order as production source

latest_license_attribution_review.ngsl_required_notice: New General Service List by Browne, C., Culligan, B., and Phillips, J. is licensed under CC BY-SA 4.0. Retrieved from https://www.newgeneralservicelist.com/new-general-service-list.
latest_license_attribution_review.cefr_j_required_notice: The CEFR-J Wordlist Version 1.6. Compiled by Yukio Tono, Tokyo University of Foreign Studies. Retrieved from https://www.cefr-j.org/download.html.

delivery_contract.workbook_title: LunaCards US English Core 3000 - A1/A2 Part 1

course_metadata_sheet.shape: row 1 has spreadsheet language codes; row 2 has Title; row 3 has Description
course_metadata_sheet.language_columns_source: first-release workbook language order from config/language-order.json, including EN-GB

workbook_columns.translation_columns_source: config/language-order.json spreadsheetCode values in exact order, including EN-GB for first release
workbook_columns.example_columns_source: config/language-order.json spreadsheetCode values in exact order prefixed with example_, including EN-GB for first release

article_display_policy.en_policy: US English countable singular nouns use a/an; plural and uncountable nouns do not get artificial a/an; verbs use to + base verb; other POS use base learner form.
article_display_policy.target_policy: Languages with learner-useful articles/gender/classifier markers use them according to language-specific rules; languages without articles do not get artificial articles.

workbook_columns.transcription_policy: Learners study English with US source pronunciation first, so only EN source word/example transcription is included. EN-GB text/example columns are included, but EN-GB transcription requires separate approval.

candidate_pool_contract.selection_policy: For the first A1/A2 pilot, avoid making the workbook mostly grammar/function words. Select at most 30 grammar-sensitive function rows; fill the rest with ordinary A1/A2 vocabulary candidates.

latest_en_transcription_review.source_method: CMUdict exact-token lookup plus deterministic ARPABET-to-IPA conversion

repairs_before_pass: core3000_0082 too repaired for HI, BN, MY, NE, TA, TE, KN and ML to preserve excessive degree
core3000_0028 will repaired for PT-BR display/example alignment

basis: focused low-resource language polish completed for MY, KM, LO, SI, SR, SL, LT, LV, IS, TA, TE, KN, HY and SW
